stories.title: Ukraine war briefing: Chinese ship hit on way in to Odesa port, says Zelenskyy
stories.summary: Civilian vessels under Russian attack in Black Sea; Kyiv condemns Belarus nuclear drills as threat to Nato and global security. What we know on day 1,546 Continue reading...

stories.summary: Democrats criticize deal as a slush fund that ‘funnels taxpayer dollars’ to president’s political allies Sign up for the Breaking News US newsletter email The justice department announced on Monday it was creating a loosely controlled and secretive $1.776bn fund to compensate Donald Trump allies as part of an agreement in which Trump and his sons dropped a $10bn longshot lawsuit against the IRS. The money, which critics said was essentially a slush fund , will be overseen by five commissioners –

stories.title: Xi prepares to welcome Putin to China four days after hosting Trump
stories.summary: Chinese state media say Beijing emerging as ‘focal point of global diplomacy’ with Russian leader arriving on Tuesday Xi Jinping and Vladimir Putin exchanged “congratulatory letters” on Sunday ahead of the Russian president’s visit to Beijing this week, four days after Donald Trump left China after a high-stakes summit . Xi said bilateral cooperation between Russia and China had “continuously deepened and solidified”, Chinese state media reported, with this year marking the 30th anniversary of t

stories.title: Sweden orders 4 frigates from French company Naval Group
stories.summary: Sweden plans to acquire four new frigates starting in 2030 in a deal worth billions. They will be the largest surface warships in Sweden's navy. The newest NATO member is rushing to build up its military capabilities.

stories.title: Reuters: Az amerikai külügyminiszter-helyettes intézte el a vízumot a volt lengyel igazságügyi miniszternek
stories.summary: A korrupcióval gyanúsított lengyel exminiszter így még Magyar Péter beiktatása előtt el tudta hagyni Magyarországot.

stories.title: BL-sztorik: síri csend volt a lidérces döntő után a Liverpool öltözőjében, de még a repülőn is hazafelé
stories.summary: Május 30-án a sorozat történetében először rendezheti meg Budapest a Bajnokok Ligája döntőjét, a Puskás Arénában ünnepelhet Európa legjobb futballcsapata. A fináléig mindennap visszatekintünk a döntők egy-egy nagy pillanatára, egyéniségére, érdekességére. BL-sztorik, tizenharmadik rész.

stories.title: Vitézy Dávid: A valaha volt legnagyobb terhelés vár a budapesti repülőtérre a BL-döntő idején
stories.summary: Ezért Pósfai Gábor belügyminiszterrel külön egyeztetést tartottak a szervezőkkel, és a BKK, a MÁV, a Budapest Airport vezetőivel is.

stories.title: Hungary's ambassador to Warsaw recalled ahead of Magyar delegation's visit
stories.summary: FM Anita Orbán said that Hungarian-Polish relations are entering a new era, and a new person will be better able to represent the renewed foreign policy direction with credibility and consistency in the period ahead.

stories.title: Nyilvánosan elérhetőek a kegyelmi ügy dokumentumai
stories.summary: A dokumentumok szerint Varga Judit nem javasolt kegyelmet K. Endrének, Novák Katalin azonban megváltoztatta a döntést. Az is kiderült, hogy Ferenc pápa budapesti látogatása miatt a Sándor-palota kérte az ügymenet felgyorsítását. A kormány szerint a magyar társadalomnak joga van megismerni, hogyan születhetett meg a döntés. Itt vannak a részletek.

stories.title: Arte: Kérdés, hogy mit tehet az EU az LMBTQ+ közösségért, ha Orbán és Fico eddig fittyet hányhatott a szabályokra
stories.summary: A szexuális kisebbségeket érő gyűlölet-bűncselekmények és erőszakos esetek egyre gyakoribbak.

stories.title: President urged expediting clemency case in 2023 citing upcoming papal visit
stories.summary: Part of the documents connected to one of the biggest scandals of the last few years in Hungary have now been released to the public, but it remains unclear why then president Novák chose to grant a presidential pardon – a decision which led to her resignation.

stories.title: Reagált az ÁSZ arra, hogy Magyar Péter 15-20 zsák ledarált iratot talált Lázár János volt minisztériumának épületében
stories.summary: A számvevőszék szerint a miniszterelnök téved, amikor arról beszél egy videón, hogy tiltott pártfinanszírozást is felvetnek a pincében talált iratok.

stories.title: Az MVM-csoport egy cége építheti a magyar-szerb olajvezetéket
stories.summary: Nem volt könnyű szülés, de végül kihirdették, hogy az egyetlen induló meg is nyerte a tendert.

stories.title: Nagyon úgy tűnik, hogy az új világrendben Európa lesz a legnagyobb préda – élő interjú Orbán Krisztiánnal
stories.summary: Milyen lesz egyáltalán az új világrend, amely a globalizáció eddig ismert formáját váltja fel? Orbán Krisztián a világgazdaság és a globális hatalmi viszonyok szemünk előtt zajló átrendeződéséről.

stories.title: Magyar Péter mától miniszterelnök-helyettesnek nevezi ki Ruff Bálintot
stories.summary: Magyar Péter azt írta, „Orbán Anita miniszterelnök-helyettes és a saját egyidejű esetleges akadályoztatásom esetére” nevezi ki Ruffot.

stories.title: Lovász László szerint érthető, hogy a kormány felmondta az Élvonal Alapítvány szerződését
stories.summary: Az MTA volt elnöke az Élvonal kuratóriumának tagja, a Telexnek azt írta: nem elegendő az, hogy egy nagyszerű, Nobel-díjas tudóshoz rendelünk magyar léptékben szinte felfoghatatlan mértékű forrásokat.

stories.title: A kormány már tárgyal az Európai Bizottsággal, de feltételt szabott Ukrajna uniós csatlakozásához
stories.summary: Magyar Péter kész Beregszászon találkozni Volodimir Zelenszkijjel, ha rendeződik az ukrajnai magyar kisebbség helyzete.

stories.title: Magyar Péter a nyár második felétől újra országjárásra indulhat
stories.summary: A miniszterelnök erről a köztársasági jogkörök bővítésével összefüggésben beszélt.

stories.title: Az önkormányzatoknak ki kell fizetniük a szolidaritási adót
stories.summary: Magyar Péter a kormányülés után mondta, hogy „jogos elvárása a kormánynak és az országnak, hogy mindenki teljesítse a jogi kötelezettségét”, és felajánlotta Budapest főpolgármesterének, hogy segít visszafogni a költségeket.

stories.title: Orbán Anita: Hazarendelem Magyarország varsói nagykövetét
stories.summary: A külügyminiszter szerint „a megújuló külpolitikai irányvonalat egy új személy tudja majd hitelesen és következetesen képviselni a következő időszakban.”

stories.title: Jönnek a kegyelmi ügy részletei, mennek a vármegyék – a kormányülés utáni sajtótájékoztató röviden
stories.summary: A maratoni sajtótájékoztatón szó volt a miniszterelnök lengyelországi és ausztriai útjáról, valamint arról is, hogy az új kormány rengeteg mindent felül fog vizsgálni.

stories.title: A streamingnek sem sikerült megölnie Magyarország legnagyobb lemezvásárát
stories.summary: A Lemezpiac a Pecsában született tradíciót viszi tovább, egyre csak növekvő látogatottsággal. Ott voltunk a születésnapján.

stories.title: Magyar: Case files of clemency case to be published
stories.summary: Speaking at a press conference, the Hungarian PM also spoke about the importance of the rights of the Hungarian minority in Ukraine, the purpose of his upcoming trip to Poland and Austria, and the priority of securing EU funds, among others, on Monday evening.

stories.title: Magyar a kegyelmi ügyről: A fideszes fiúk előszeretettel bújnak nők szoknyája mögé
stories.summary: Magyar Péter bejelentette, hogy kedden hozzák nyilvánosságra a kegyelmi ügy dossziéját. Ebben nincs benne, hogy Varga Judit miért írta alá a megváltoztatott döntést. A miniszterelnök szerint a fő felelős az, aki rávette Novák Katalint, hogy adjon kegyelmet.

stories.title: Kapu Tibor: Magyarország kutatóűrhajósa vagyok, nem a Fidesz űrhajósa, és nem is leszek a Tisza űrhajósa
stories.summary: Kapu szerint egy magyar űrhajósnak nem szabad pártpolitizálnia.

stories.title: Hétfő esti Magyar Közlöny: jönnek a soron kívüli vizsgálatok, szűkös határidőket kaptak a miniszterek
stories.summary: A dokumentumban a közigazgatási államtitkárok kinevezése mellett számos olyan ügy is szerepel, amikről a kormányülés utáni sajtótájékoztatón is szó volt.

stories.title: Young Czechs drink less beer but risk other addictions
stories.summary: The Czech Republic, the beer world champion, has seen a record decline in consumption. Younger generations are drinking less. Breweries are worried, but health experts say it's good news.

stories.title: Could Romania and Moldova reunite?
stories.summary: Moldovan leader Maia Sandu and her Romanian counterpart Nicusor Dan want to reunite their countries. Is that a realistic scenario?

stories.title: Latvia: Prime Minister Evika Silina resigns over handling of drone incidents after coalition ally's withdrawal
stories.summary: Evika Silina says she is resigning her post, following the defense minister's resignation and the coalition government's collapse amid controversy over Ukrainian drones aimed at Russia that landed in the Baltic country.

stories.title: Bolivia: Clashes in La Paz as protesters demand President Paz's ouster
stories.summary: Riot police fired tear gas at demonstrators in Bolivia's capital, La Paz, while road blockades deepened shortages of fuel, food and medical supplies.

stories.title: Yes, Trump might really attack Cuba
stories.summary: The Trump administration has been dancing around the question of whether it will carry out military strikes against Cuba. I am told it is increasingly willing to take such a step. That’s a significant escalation from a few months ago, when officials were primarily focused on using economic and diplomatic pressure to squeeze the communist regime in Havana. stories.title: Mark Fuhrman, LA detective convicted of lying at OJ Simpson trial, dies
stories.summary: Fuhrman, who found bloody glove at Simpson’s home, charged with perjury after audio revealed use of racial slurs Former Los Angeles police detective Mark Fuhrman, who was convicted of lying during testimony at the OJ Simpson murder trial, has died. Fuhrman was one of the first two police detectives sent to investigate the 1994 killings of Simpson’s ex-wife Nicole Brown Simpson and her friend, Ronald Goldman, in Los Angeles. stories.title: From sanctioned cars to beauty clinics, Russian rubles have flowed into China’s border towns since Ukraine war
stories.summary: Suifenhe, a small city in China’s economically depressed rust belt, is a microcosm of an evolving Chinese-Russian trading relationship Suited and booted in a navy twinset tracksuit and colourful high-top trainers, Wang Runguo is hustling. Darting across the gleaming floors of his cavernous car showroom, the 45-year-old from one of China’s poorest provinces is closing on yet another deal. stories.title: Moscow begins nuclear drills as Russia-Ukraine drone strikes intensify
stories.summary: As Russian and Ukrainian forces carried out fresh cross-border aerial strikes, Moscow launched nationwide nuclear drills.

stories.summary: Oil prices fell on Tuesday morning as Donald Trump announced he was calling off a planned military attack on Iran.

stories.title: Ex-commando Al Carns ‘keeping options open’ for challenge to Keir Starmer
stories.summary: LONDON — A little-known British defense minister is being urged by MPs to consider a bid to replace Keir Starmer, as a leadership crisis consumes the governing Labour Party. Al Carns, the minister for the armed forces, is being touted by supporters who say the party now needs a radical change if it is to win back thousands of voters who dumped Labour for Nigel Farage’s Reform UK or the left-wing Greens at recent local elections. Carns, who only entered parliament as the MP for the Birmingh

stories.title: Aid cuts and war complicate Ebola response in Congo
stories.summary: Deep foreign aid cuts allowed the deadly Ebola virus to spread undetected in the Democratic Republic of Congo and Uganda, while ongoing conflict has complicated efforts to build resilient health care services in the region, experts warned Monday. Spanish Prime Minister Pedro Sánchez framed the crisis as a matter of security and justice at the World Health Organization’s annual meeting in Geneva, where the pressing question for health leaders is how to continue funding global health amid cu

stories.title: Trump’s shifting remarks on Taiwan are perfect for China to exploit
stories.summary: President’s remarks about Taiwan arms deals being a ‘negotiating chip’ with Beijing have been seized on by Chinese state media It has been an unsettling few days for Taiwan’s government. When Donald Trump met Xi Jinping in Beijing on Thursday, many feared the unpredictable US leader could upend Washington’s longstanding support for Taipei. But beyond a starkly worded statement from Xi stressing China’s claims over Taiwan, which it claims as part of its territory despite never having ruled it, in

stories.title: Experts locate bodies of four missing Italian divers inside Maldives cave
stories.summary: Spokesperson for Indian Ocean island nation says they will try to recover explorers in next couple of days Rescuers have located the bodies of four Italian divers deep inside an underwater cave in an atoll in the Maldives, four days after they were reported missing. Searches had resumed after being suspended following the death of a local military diver during a perilous mission to try to reach them. Continue reading...

stories.summary: Port has upgraded offshore wind facilities and is to expand quays, ferry terminals and cruise ship services The operator of Belfast harbour plans to spend £1.3bn over the next 25 years to take advantage of strong economic growth in Northern Ireland, in what would be one of the largest non-governmental investments in the region’s history. The Belfast Harbour Commissioners said the money would be spent on upgrading the port, with the possibility of residential property developments that could add

stories.title: ‘Huge milestone’ as Libyan militia commander accused of torture to appear at ICC
stories.summary: Prosecution is seen as landmark step towards justice over abuses of refugees trying to reach Europe from Africa A former militia commander accused of overseeing murder, rape, enslavement and torture in Libyan detention centres will appear at the international criminal court on Tuesday for a hearing that campaigners say is a landmark step towards “justice, truth, reparation and deterrence” of abuses of refugees trying to reach Europe from Africa. stories.title: Standard Chartered to cut more than 7,000 jobs as it steps up AI use
stories.summary: London-headquartered bank will reduce back-office jobs and aims to move some workers to new roles Business live – latest updates Standard Chartered plans to cut more than 7,000 jobs over the next four years as it increasingly uses artificial intelligence. The London-headquartered lender is one of the first major global banks to lay out plans to cut thousands of jobs, citing AI as a driver to make its operations slimmer as it seeks to increase its profitability and tackle competition. stories.summary: Leader of the Venstre party, Troels Lund Poulsen, hoping to govern with support of the Conservatives and the Liberal Alliance Since Denmark’s outgoing foreign minister Lars Løkke Rasmussen will play a defining role in deciding it Poulsen’s ambitious arrangement could even work, let’s bring you his profile by our Nordic correspondent, Miranda Bryant. The European Commission has been asked about its response to the renewed talk about Britain’s potential future attempt to rejoin the European Union – but did not take a bait. stories.summary: The US president says plans for a US military strike on Iran have been paused because ‘serious negotiations are now taking place’ Iran has made a new proposal for a deal to definitively end the war in the Middle East, officials in the region said on Monday, with Donald Trump claiming he had postponed new military strikes so talks could continue. stories.title: Trump says he paused attack on Iran at Gulf leaders' request
stories.summary: President Donald Trump said he called off a planned attack on Iran after the UAE, Saudi Arabia and Qatar asked Washington to suspend strikes.

stories.title: How China became Russia's economic lifeline
stories.summary: Hot on the heels of Donald Trump's visit, Russian President Vladimir Putin travels to Beijing for talks with Xi Jinping. DW explores how China's trade and financial support is helping keep Russia's economy afloat.

stories.summary: Three people are still missing after a house collapsed in the eastern German city of Görlitz on Monday evening, while a study has found that one in five German gas stations have breached pricing laws. DW has more.

The U.S. plan to further strike Iran was scheduled for Tuesday, Trump  wrote on Truth Social . He met with his national security team over the weekend after no movement on a deal with Iran, and  threatened on Sunday  that Iran “better get moving, FAST, or there won’t be anything left of them.” The president said he reconsidered the strikes after requests made

stories.title: Nancy Pelosi breaks silence to endorse San Francisco supervisor as successor
stories.summary: Former House speaker, who had not yet weighed in, declared Connie Chan ‘best prepared’ to represent city Nancy Pelosi on Monday endorsed Connie Chan , a San Francisco supervisor, in the race to succeed her as the city’s representative in Congress, calling her the candidate who “stands above the rest”. stories.title: Energy bills will rise by £209 in July to £1,850 a year, forecast says
stories.summary: Cornwall Insight predicts rise in price cap of nearly 13% in Great Britain as Iran war pushes up gas costs Business live – latest updates Energy bills for households in Great Britain could increase by more than £200 a year to almost £1,900 from this summer in “a kick in the teeth” for millions struggling with the cost of living crisis. stories.title: The French are hitting their protein goals - thanks to a cheese that looks like ectoplasm | Emma Beddington
stories.summary: La cancoillotte, a low-fat liquid cheese product, has somehow taken the country of haute cuisine by storm Exciting news from over the Channel: a viral cheese has dropped, but good luck spelling, or saying, it. stories.title: Could the UK really rejoin the EU? - The Latest
stories.summary: The Brexit debate has been reignited after Labour leadership contender Wes Streeting called it a ‘catastrophic mistake’ and said the UK should rejoin the European Union. His comments put pressure on rival Andy Burnham, who has previously advocated for rejoining the bloc but is fighting a byelection in the leave-voting Makerfield constituency. But how would rejoining work and would the EU even agree to it? Lucy Hough speaks to the Guardian’s Europe correspondent, Jon Henley Continue reading...

stories.title: ‘Disposable’ operatives for hire are a new menace for western countries

stories.title: Updates: 3 killed in shooting at San Diego's largest mosque

stories.summary: Teenage gunmen have shot dead three men at the Islamic Center of San Diego before killing themselves. The incident is being treated as a hate crime by investigators.

stories.title: India news: Petrol, diesel prices hiked again as Iran crisis deepens
stories.summary: India's state-run fuel retailers hiked fuel prices for the second time in a week with no end in sight for the Iran conflict. Meanwhile, PM Narendra Modi will attend the India-Nordic Summit in Norway. DW has more.

stories.title: China's moment? Putin heads to Beijing after Trump courts Xi
stories.summary: Just days after hosting Donald Trump, China's Xi Jinping is set to welcome Russian leader Vladimir Putin. The meetings underscore Beijing's growing role in a fractured geopolitical landscape.

stories.title: Top European Parliament honor to be awarded for first time

stories.title: Iran sends revised US proposal, Trump cancels planned strike

Ziel sei die Stärkung des Standortes Deutschland sowie die langfristige Sicherung des Zugangs zu einer hochwertig

stories.title: Drone strike sparks fire near UAE nuclear plant amid Iran ceasefire tension
stories.summary: A drone strike sparked a fire near the UAE’s Barakah nuclear plant, officials say, with no injuries or radiation leak reported as regional tensions around the Iran ceasefire escalate.

It was the second such painting of the rare amphibian he had walked past that morning. In recent weeks he had seen axolotl murals pop up in neighbourhoods across Mexico City. “It’s a waste of money,” he said. “You could use that budget for fixing potholes, traffic lights, security cameras. stories.title: No ‘tailor-made’ deal for UK if it wants to rejoin bloc, say former EU Brexit officials

stories.title: Spain has banned Francoist symbols. So why are there still kitsch cafes glorifying the dictator? | Abbas Asaria
stories.summary: These unsettling and unusual places tell a vivid story about the unique way that Spain deals with its past – or fails to Una Grande Libre reads the sign above the entrance to a bar-restaurant in Madrid’s Usera neighbourhood. This was Francisco Franco’s motto for Spain – one, great, free – and it is accompanied by a large portrait of the dictator superimposed on to the window. stories.title: Mága Zoltán kért 500 milliót a minisztériumtól, hogy a Fidesznek kampányoljon, Hankóék odaszóltak az NKA-nak, hogy teljesítsék
stories.summary: A „nemzeti oldal” választási győzelme szempontjából kulcsfontosságú társadalmi csoportok, például a cigány szavazók megszólítására kért félmilliárd forintot Mága, amit aztán az Nemzeti Kulturális Alap pályázatkezelője el is utalt a titokzatos keretből. A nyerő pályázatot beadó Novo Studium cég ügyvezetője azt állítja, nem ő írta a kérvényt.

stories.title: Mutatjuk, kiket neveztek ki közigazgatási államtitkárnak az új kormányban
stories.summary: Az Oktatási és Gyermekügyi Minisztérium oktatási államtitkárát még nem közölték.

stories.title: Tarr Zoltán elrendelte a Nemzeti Kulturális Alap kifizetéseinek felfüggesztését
stories.summary: A miniszter azt mondta, a hátralevő kifizetések listáját átnézik majd.

stories.title: Legkevesebb hárman meghaltak egy lövöldözésben San Diego legnagyobb mecsetében
stories.summary: A hatóságok azt tanácsolták a lakosságnak, hogy kerüljék el a környéket.

stories.title: German singer Heino pushes back against the far-right AfD
stories.summary: A far‑right politician in Germany used schlager icon Heino in election advertising without his consent. Heino is now demanding compensation for pain and suffering.

stories.title: Ebola in Africa: Why it's a constant threat
stories.summary: Ebola is highly contagious and deadly. It spreads internationally, such as in the May 2026 outbreaks in the Democratic Republic of the Congo and Uganda. But that doesn't automatically make it a pandemic.

stories.title: Elversberg: Bundesliga newcomers from tiny town make history
stories.summary: A team from a small south-western German town of just 7,000 inhabitants has made history by sealing promotion to become the 59th club to play in the Bundesliga.

stories.title: Eurovision winner Dara arrives to screaming fans in Bulgaria
stories.summary: The 27-year-old's tune Bangaranga won Bulgaria its first ever title in the song contest.

stories.title: World Urban Forum opens in Baku with warning on global housing divide
stories.summary: Ministers and experts opened the World Urban Forum in Baku on Sunday with stark warnings about the global housing divide, calling for international cooperation to tackle a growing crisis.

stories.title: From escaping child marriage 'to an old pervert' to becoming Sierra Leone's first lady
stories.summary: The private world of one of Africa's most powerful political women is revealed in a BBC World Service exclusive.

stories.title: Inside the 'kill-zone' on Ukraine's front line, where new weapons have transformed war
stories.summary: Machines are increasingly replacing humans on the front line, but troops are still vital for defending territory.

stories.title: 'This may be the last time you hear my voice': Political executions surge in Iran since start of war
stories.summary: Since the US and Israel attacked Iran on 28 February, the UN says it’s verified the execution of at least 32 political prisoners.

stories.title: Moment two fighter jets collide mid-air at US air show
stories.summary: Four crew members safely ejected from US Navy jets and are in a stable condition after the collision.

stories.title: Taiwan will not provoke conflict nor give up sovereignty, says president
stories.summary: Lai Ching-te made his first direct response to Trump and Xi's meeting where they discussed Taiwan independence.

stories.title: Iran has responded to latest US peace proposal, foreign ministry official says
stories.summary: On Sunday, Iran's Fars news agency said Washington had presented a five-point list, which included a demand for Iran to keep only one nuclear site in operation and transfer its stockpile of highly enriched uranium to the US.

stories.title: Taiwan’s president defends US arms purchases that Trump called 'bargaining chip'
stories.summary: Trump’s calling of arms sales to Taiwan a “bargaining chip” has raised concerns on the self-governing island of a potential change in US foreign policy towards the territory after the US president’s latest visit to China.

stories.title: 'Everest Man' and 'Mountain Queen' break own records scaling world's tallest peak
stories.summary: Kami Rita Sherpa and Lakpa Sherpa summited Everest for the 32nd and 11th time, respectively.

stories.title: North Korea's Kim calls on military to bolster frontline defences against 'arch enemy'
stories.summary: The plans come at a low point in relations with South Korea and Pyongyang unresponsive to Seoul's repeated offers for dialogue.

stories.title: Driver of crashed train tested positive for drugs, Thai police say
stories.summary: The freight train collided with a bus in Bangkok, killing eight people and injuring dozens of others.

stories.title: Watch: Bulgaria welcomes home Eurovision winner Dara with trophy
stories.summary: Eurovision winner Dara returned to Sofia to cheering fans after securing Bulgaria's first-ever victory at the song contest in Vienna.

stories.title: Japan arrests US nationals after one entered enclosure at viral baby monkey Punch's zoo
stories.summary: The baby monkey became an internet star this year after the zoo posted photos of Punch clutching an IKEA plush orangutan for comfort after being rejected by his mother.

stories.title: Bodybuilders show off muscles at Southeast Asian championships
stories.summary: Myanmar hosts the Southeast Asian Bodybuilding Championships in Yangon, with athletes from eight countries competing from May 14 to 18.

stories.title: Arrest of Iraqi terror suspect with alleged links to Iran’s Quds Force is astonishing but not surprising
stories.summary: Iran has a long history of unconventional operations, all designed to divert, distract and destabilise current or potential enemies The arrest by US authorities of an alleged Iraqi commander of an Iranian-backed militia group now accused of responsibility for 18 terrorist attacks in the UK, Europe and Canada since the beginning of the Iran war is an astonishing development – yet not the least bit surprising. According to a complaint unsealed on Friday in a federal court in Manhattan, Mohammad Ba

stories.title: ‘Feels like an illusion’: inside post-Maduro Venezuela’s bewildering new era
stories.summary: Four months after Trump’s surprise raid, a political thaw has descended – but mingled with hope is trepidation for what comes next When Ángel Linares heard a strange buzz followed by an explosion, his first thought was that neighbours were setting off fireworks to celebrate the new year. Then his windows shattered, the building’s walls shook and its facade was ripped off, sending him flying on to the ground of an apartment suddenly reduced to rubble. His 85-year-old mother, Jesucita, feared Vene

stories.title: 'From collection to connection': How museums are redefining themselves
stories.summary: Gone are the days when museums were just places to see art and artifacts. On International Museum Day, DW looks at how museums are becoming unique spaces of community engagement and participation.

stories.title: Germany's housing crisis hits immigrants hardest
stories.summary: The German housing market crisis is deepening social inequality. Newcomers in particular are struggling to find affordable housing — with consequences for integration, education, and the labor market.

stories.title: Germany stakes claim in new space race
stories.summary: German companies of all sizes and all over the country are developing satellites and space applications. They want to be part of the lucrative industry, which is increasingly crucial for civilian and military purposes.

stories.title: Germany news: UNICEF says child well-being ranking 'alarming'
stories.summary: A new study ranks Germany near the bottom in child well-being and education. Meanwhile, an escaped tiger was killed by police after injuring a man near Leipzig. DW has more.

stories.title: Dead humpback whale off Denmark is 'Timmy'
stories.summary: Divers have confirmed that a dead humpback whale spotted off Denmark is the same creature that spent weeks beached off Germany's Baltic coast. It comes two weeks after a contentious rescue operation.

stories.title: Putin to visit China days after Trump's Beijing trip, Kremlin says
stories.summary: Putin and Chinese President Xi Jinping will discuss ways to deepen their countries' "comprehensive partnership and strategic cooperation," the Kremlin said.

stories.title: London on edge as Tommy Robinson backers and pro-Palestinian marchers rally
stories.summary: Tens of thousands of people joined rival London marches for Tommy Robinson and Palestine, as 4,000 officers used strict routes to avoid clashes and keep order.

stories.title: South Korea: Thousands watch robot monks march through Seoul’s historic streets
stories.summary: Four robot monks in traditional robes joined Seoul’s lantern parade ahead of Buddha’s birthday, symbolising Buddhism’s tech-savvy modernisation.
